source.title: Hierarchically organized behavior and its neural foundations: A reinforcement learning perspective

confidence_rationale: This is the canonical bridge paper between options/HRL formalism and biological-substrate mapping. It maps options-framework primitives (option, sub-policy, termination, policy-over-options) to dorsolateral PFC, OFC, basal-ganglia, and dopamine signals. For Pull 4's vocabulary-mapping question this is the load-bearing paper showing that the AI options vocabulary already has a worked-out neural mapping -- exactly what REE's rule-apprehension cluster has been independently sketching. Confidence not higher because the neural mapping is suggestive rather than mechanistic in the EXQ-substrate-validation sense, and several of the predictions remain debated (especially the OFC role).
failure_signatures: If REE's MECH-318 (rule-state-abstraction) cannot be plausibly identified with the OFC role Botvinick 2009 proposes, the inheritance pathway from this paper to MECH-318 is weak.

If REE's ARC-062 cannot be mapped to the dorsolateral-PFC policy-over-options role this paper anchors, the gated_policy interpretation as option-selection machinery is in tension with the canonical neural reading.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Hierarchical reinforcement learning (HRL), built on the options framework, maps onto biological-substrate components: options/sub-policies are encoded in dorsolateral PFC, the policy-over-options sits in dorsolateral or anterior PFC, OFC encodes the option-conditioned state abstraction needed to evaluate option-level value, and the basal ganglia + dopamine implement the gating and learning signals. Hierarchical structure in human and animal behaviour can be parsimoniously accounted for by this option-augmented RL architecture.
mapping.ree_translation: Botvinick, Niv & Barto's neural mapping IS the mapping REE has independently sketched for ARC-062 + ARC-064 + MECH-317 + MECH-318. ARC-062's gated_policy + discriminator is the dorsolateral-PFC policy-over-options. ARC-064's bottom-up rule discovery is the option-discovery problem in HRL. MECH-317's action-chunking is the option-formation pathway in dorsolateral striatum. MECH-318's rule-state-abstraction is the OFC-cognitive-map role this paper anchors. The vocabulary equivalence is essentially complete for the *cluster level*. Where REE diverges is in the specific sub-mechanisms (especially MECH-094 simulation-mode write-gating, dual nociceptive streams, and V_s invalidation) -- those are not in HRL canon.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The Botvinick 2009 mapping is a theoretical proposal more than an experimentally-cemented mechanism. Specifically: the OFC-cognitive-map role is now better-anchored by Wilson 2014, Schuck 2016, Niv 2019 (already in Pull 2's set); the dorsolateral-PFC policy-over-options claim is still under empirical refinement; and the basal-ganglia gating-signal role is well-established but the specific HRL reading (vs other gating accounts) is not uniquely supported. So adopting HRL vocabulary commits REE to a *family* of compatible neural mappings rather than a single locked-down assignment.
mapping.source_context: Theoretical review article in Cognition (Cell Press), with no direct experimental data, that synthesizes the AI options/HRL literature with the cognitive-neuroscience PFC-action-hierarchy literature.

confidence_components.notes: Top-tier interdisciplinary venue (Cognition), highly cited (1500+), Princeton-Amherst lineage. Mapping fidelity is high because this paper does the cluster-level translation REE has been independently constructing. Transfer risk is moderate because the neural assignments are theoretical rather than mechanistically locked-down and have evolved since 2009.
